project_title: Library Book Borrowing and Reservation System

original_input.project_title: Library Book Borrowing and Reservation System
original_input.requirements_text: Members should be able to search for books by title, author, or ISBN. Members should be able to borrow available books, reserve unavailable books, and view their loans, due dates, and reservations. Librarians should be able to add new books, update book records, mark books as borrowed or returned, manage overdue items, and remove books from the catalogue. The system should prevent members from borrowing more than five books. Notifications should be sent for available reservations and overdue books. User authentication is required. The system should keep a record of key actions. Search results should be returned within 3 seconds under normal usage, and the interface should be intuitive for all user roles.

requirements_output.project_title: Library Book Borrowing and Reservation System

functional_requirements.statement: Members can search for books by title, author, or ISBN.
acceptance_criteria: Search results are returned when a user inputs a title, author, or ISBN.
A book's availability status changes to borrowed after a member borrows it.
A reservation is created when a member reserves an unavailable book.

functional_requirements.statement: Members can view their loans, due dates, and reservations.
acceptance_criteria: Users can access a list of current loans with due dates and reservations.
A new book record is created in the system when a librarian adds a book.
An existing book record is updated when modified by a librarian.

functional_requirements.statement: Librarians can mark books as borrowed or returned.
acceptance_criteria: The status of a book changes to borrowed or available in the system.
The system generates a report of overdue books accessible to librarians.

functional_requirements.statement: Members can borrow up to five books.
acceptance_criteria: A member cannot borrow more than five books simultaneously.

functional_requirements.statement: Notifications are sent for available reservations and overdue books.
acceptance_criteria: Members receive notifications when a reserved book is available or if books are overdue.
Users must log in to access the system, with successful authentication.

functional_requirements.statement: System keeps a record of key actions.
acceptance_criteria: A log of actions like borrowing, returning, and reserving is maintained.

non_functional_requirements.statement: Search results should be returned within 3 seconds under normal usage.
The interface should be intuitive for all user roles.

assumptions: System has a stable internet connection for cloud-based features.
Members have unique identifiers for authentication purposes.
clarification_questions: What details should be logged in the record of key actions?
What is the expected volume of search requests under normal usage?

glossary.definition: The action of a member taking a book for a period of time.
A request by a member to hold a book that is currently unavailable.
A status indicating a book has not been returned by its due date.

code_generation_output.project_title: Library Book Borrowing and Reservation System

files.purpose: Project overview, setup instructions, and usage guide
files.content:
# Library Book Borrowing and Reservation System

## Overview
This project is a Flask-based application for managing book borrowing and reservations in a library.

## Setup Instructions
1. Clone the repository.
2. Install dependencies using `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
3. Run the application with `python app.py`.

## Usage
Access the application at `http://localhost:5000`.

files.purpose: Application entry point with Flask app initialization
files.content:
from flask import Flask
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
from flask_login import LoginManager

app = Flask(__name__)
app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///library.db'
db = SQLAlchemy(app)
login_manager = LoginManager(app)

# Import routes
from app.routes import *

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

from app import db

class Book(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    title = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    author = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    isbn = db.Column(db.String(20), unique=True, nullable=False)
    is_borrowed = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False)

class Member(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = db.Column(db.String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
    loans = db.relationship('Loan', backref='member', lazy=True)

class Loan(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    book_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('book.id'), nullable=False)
    member_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('member.id'), nullable=False)
    due_date = db.Column(db.Date, nullable=False)

deployment_output.dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY . /app
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
EXPOSE 5000
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
